fields.name: device_status_id
fields.title: Device status ID
fields.description: Unique identifier for this device status record.

fields.name: service_date
fields.title: Service date
fields.description: Service date associated with this status record. Optional because device status may not align with transit service dates.

fields.name: record_timestamp
fields.title: Record timestamp
fields.description: Timestamp when this status record was created in the system. May differ from status_change_timestamp if status changes are recorded retroactively.

fields.name: status_change_timestamp
fields.title: Status change timestamp
fields.description: Timestamp when the device status actually changed. This is the time the device went offline, came back online, or experienced the status change.

fields.name: device_id
fields.title: ID referencing devices.device_id
fields.description: Identifies the device. References the devices table.

fields.name: status_type
fields.title: Status type
fields.description: Type of device status. Examples: online, offline, maintenance, error, degraded. Agencies may define additional status types as needed.

fields.name: status_reason
fields.title: Status reason
fields.description: Optional reason or description for the status change. Examples: scheduled maintenance, power failure, communication error, sensor malfunction.

name: device_status.schema.json
description: Records device operational status changes over time. This table tracks when devices (APCs, fare gates, AVL units, etc.) go offline, return to service, or experience errors. Enables data quality analysis by distinguishing between missing data due to device outages versus actual service patterns.
